patch.value:
## Observed (2026-07-20, this repository)
`pm config set ids_token_length 6` against a sparse tracked settings.json produced a 128-insertion diff: every implicit default materialized (locks.wait_ms, checkpoints, history.compact_policy, full extensions.policy block, telemetry block incl. endpoint, context sections, schema block, search rerank/query_expansion, vector_store.collection_name...), and the explicitly stored `validation.parent_reference: "warn"` (a VALID enum per settings-validator vLiteral("warn","strict_error")) was replaced with "strict_error" in the same write.

## Why it matters
1. Frozen defaults: materialized values no longer track future default changes (e.g. telemetry endpoint churn never reaches the workspace) — the file silently pins today's defaults.
2. Multi-agent conflict surface: a fully materialized singleton file maximizes cross-branch overlap exactly where pm-xdn6's config merge driver has no per-key semantics; two branches each touching one unrelated key now conflict across the whole file.
3. Intent loss: explicit stored values flattened to effective/normalized values (warn -> strict_error, likely via governance layering writing the effective value back) destroy the user's recorded choice even when runtime behavior was already equivalent.

## Direction
Sparse persistence: write only keys that differ from defaults (or at minimum only the mutated key), keep normalization read-side, and add a round-trip regression spec.
pm config set on one leaf key rewrites settings.json with every default materialized and silently replaces explicit stored values (validation.parent_reference warn -> strict_error)

Setting one config key persists only that key's change (or a documented normalize step); explicit stored enum values survive unrelated writes; a regression spec round-trips a sparse settings.json through config set and asserts no unrelated key changes
